text.text:
This place is objectively delicious.

I came here at a 5pm seating on a Saturday. They were already a few people waiting for the place to open. The restaurant filled up quickly. Geoff and the whole team were there. Rachel was our server. From start to finish the service was excellent. They filled my water and checked it on me.

We had the peanuts, cornbread, greens, bbq shrimp, salmon crudo, jidori chicken, the lamb, and the strawberry cobbler. All dishes were very tasty. The bbq shrimp, chicken, and cornbread were the highlights. If you are on the fence about this place like I was, please try for yourself and be pleasantly delighted.

text.text:
We ordered liver and waffles, which separately kind of works but together tastes heavily like bile because of the metallic, acidity and high fat and sugar. Then we had lamb, grits, and dandalion, all are extremely salty and I do mean extremely. I normally have quite the appetite, but I couldn’t eat after a few bites and quickly was feeling a heartburn due to the high sodium content. We tried rescuing ourselves by ordering the cornbread. Cornbread was okay but not enough to even out the saltiness the rest dishes have provided especially if you unfortunately got a salt flaked bite of the butter.

My friend and I were very confused wondering if we were just eating too healthy recently but remembered only 2 days ago went to another equivalently priced/nice ambiance restaurant and weren’t shocked by the salt content.

Burdell was a very beautiful restaurant with beautiful lighting and the food is beautiful, but left us really confused and questioning our own tastebuds. The staff didn’t seem to know what to do with our feedback either. We spent $170 for two including an automatic 20% tip in the bill. The chef apparently checked the food and found they tasted normal. So unless you have lost your taste to COVID, might be too much salt to bear. I wanted dessert but also were too scared to order and went next door for cake.

text.text:
First time at Burdell and overall the food was good but the overall didn't wow me... perhaps since I had set a high expectation considering the accolades given to Burdell.

The salad, and the oxtail fries, were tasty! They were on point and I liked these. No problem with them at all.

The mustard green side was on the saltier side but I manage by eating it with more of the mixed rice and beans which was delicious with a nice spice to it.

The main entree, the pickle brine fried chicken was ok. The chicken was crispy and juicy but 1) not all pieces were marinated well as two pieces we had lacked flavor so it wasn't just how the chicken was, but inconsistency in marination, and 2) some pieces were overcooked leading a thick dry outer layer before you get into the nice juicy meat inside. Pieces that were marinated well were phenomenal along with the house made hot sauce, but due to inconsistencies, the overall was underwhelming.

Service could have been better - primarily because when our water cups were empty, I expected them to fill it or ask to fill it but they came by twice but never asked or did so, until I asked for the water cups to be refilled.

The ambiance of the place was on the upscale feeling though definitely did very well in keeping the southern feel to it with the dining wares used, and the decor.

Maybe I'll give it another try during their non pre-fixe menu and see if the experience is better.

text.text:
Made a reservation for a Wednesday night and it was packed! The place was a bit loud, which made it hard to hear and have a conversation. We got a table seat and could hear the different conversations on either side of us.

Food wise, we order the family menu that had the duck as the main entré. My favorite of the menu were the cornbread and the duck; everything else was a tad too salty or messy (like the boiled peanuts with the shell on). For a party of 2, they served three slices of the duck. Everything came in portions of 2 except the duck. Two people finished all the food and no leftover.

The food was pretty pricey and they added 20% gratuity charge. The restaurant definitely has a fine dining price tag, but lacking in the fine dining experience and taste.

text.text: Went here for brunch, and as a vegetarian they had quite a decent amount of options on the spread. Loved their avocado toast and buttermilk pancakes. They are also family friendly, we went in with a big stroller that they could accommodate. The only downside is that this is not located in best area in oakland, and has no parking. Parking is on the street, and need to be super cautious on where we keep on the streets.
